name: Mermaid CLI
one_liner: Open-source command-line tool (mmdc) that renders Mermaid diagram definitions into SVG/PNG/PDF; distributed as an npm package and Docker image under MIT license.
access_notes: Fully free and open source (MIT). Installed via npm (@mermaid-js/mermaid-cli), Docker (minlag/mermaid-cli), or npx. No account, key, or payment required. Runs entirely locally.
api_notes: No HTTP API. Exposes a CLI binary (mmdc) with input/output/theme/format flags, plus a Node.js programmatic API (importable run function). Effectively one core operation: render a Mermaid definition to an image. Agent-callable as a local CLI skill / self-hosted tool.
mcp_notes: No vendor-shipped MCP server. Several community MCP servers wrap mmdc: hustcc/mcp-mermaid, andrewginns/mermaid-mcp-server, ryu1maniwa/mermaid-local-mcp, codingthefuturewithai/mcp_mermaid_image_gen. None are published by the mermaid-js org.
main_blocker: No HTTP API — integration is via local CLI/Node API rather than a hosted service, so a Composio tool would need to shell out to mmdc (requires Node/Chromium/Puppeteer runtime) rather than call a remote endpoint.

evidence.claim: mermaid-cli (mmdc) is a CLI that takes a mermaid definition file as input and generates SVG/PNG/PDF; installed via npm/Docker/npx; MIT licensed; provides a Node.js run() API; no HTTP API or auth documented.
Community MCP servers wrap the official mermaid-cli (e.g., andrewginns/mermaid-mcp-server, ryu1maniwa/mermaid-local-mcp), confirming no official vendor MCP server exists.

open_questions: None significant. The mermaid.ink / mermaid.live hosted rendering services are separate from mermaid-cli; if a hosted HTTP render endpoint is desired, those would be evaluated separately.
